CustomFunction.name: TriggersAreActive
CustomFunction.Calculation: /** * ===================================== * TriggersAreActive * https://github.com/jbante/FileMaker-Techniques/blob/master/CustomFunctions/Triggers/TriggersAreActive.fmfn * * PURPOSE: * TriggersAreActive checks a global variable to identify whether or not * script triggers should be allowed to run. This function is best used in * concert with the TriggersDisable, TriggersEnable, and TriggersReset * functions. * * RETURNS: * False (0) if triggers should be suppressed; True (1) otherwise. * * PARAMETERS: none * * REFERENCED VARIABLES: * $$~DISABLETRIGGERS (deprecated) * $$~TRIGGERS_DISABLE * * DEPENDENCIES: none * * EXAMPLE (triggered script): * If [not TriggersAreActive] * Exit Script [True // allow triggering event to proceed] * End If * * RELEASE: 2013-04-08 * * REFERENCES: * Supressible Triggered Scripts Best Practice: http://filemakerstandards.org/display/bp/Suppressible+Triggered+Scripts * ===================================== */ not ( $$~TRIGGERS_DISABLE or $$~DISABLETRIGGERS )

CustomFunction.name: TriggersDisable
CustomFunction.Calculation: /** * ===================================== * TriggersDisable * https://github.com/jbante/FileMaker-Techniques/blob/master/CustomFunctions/Triggers/TriggersDisable.fmfn * * PURPOSE: * TriggersDisable sets global variables to indicate that the current * script is suppressing triggers. This script must be called from the * context of a script; otherwise, it will not suppress triggers, and it * will return False. In order to be suppressed, a script called via * trigger should use the TriggersAreActive function to decide whether or * not to run. * * RETURNS: * True (1) if called from the context of a script, and therefore triggers * have been suppressed; False (0) otherwise. * * PARAMETERS: none * * REFERENCED VARIABLES: * $$~DISABLETRIGGERS (deprecated) * $$~TRIGGERS_DISABLE * $$~TRIGGERS_SCRIPTS * $~triggers_disableKey * * DEPENDENCIES: none * * EXAMPLE (script): * Set Variable [$!; Value:TriggersDisable] * # Do something * Set Variable [$!; Value:TriggersEnable] * * NOTES: * This function should *always* be used in pairs with the TriggersEnable * function. * * RELEASE: 2013-04-08 * * REFERENCES: * Supressible Triggered Scripts Best Practice: http://filemakerstandards.org/display/bp/Suppressible+Triggered+Scripts * ===================================== */ Let ( [ ~scriptName = Get ( ScriptName ) ; ~scriptIsRunning = not IsEmpty ( Get ( ScriptName ) ) ; ~disableIsRunning = not IsEmpty ( $~triggers_disableKey ) ; $~triggers_disableKey = Case ( ~disableIsRunning ; $~triggers_disableKey ; ~scriptIsRunning ; Get ( UUID ) ) ; $$~TRIGGERS_SCRIPTS = If ( ~scriptIsRunning and not ~disableIsRunning ; ~scriptName & Char ( 9 ) // tab & $~triggers_disableKey & ¶ & $$~TRIGGERS_SCRIPTS ; /* Else */ $$~TRIGGERS_SCRIPTS ) ; $$~TRIGGERS_DISABLE = If ( ~scriptIsRunning ; True ; /* Else */ $$~TRIGGERS_DISABLE ) ; $$~DISABLETRIGGERS = $$~TRIGGERS_DISABLE ] ; ~scriptIsRunning )

CustomFunction.name: TriggersEnable
TriggersReset
CustomFunction.Calculation: /** * ===================================== * TriggersReset * https://github.com/jbante/FileMaker-Techniques/blob/master/CustomFunctions/Triggers/TriggersReset.fmfn * * PURPOSE: * TriggersReset sets global variables to indicate that script triggers * should be allowed to run, regardless of any persisting instructions to * the contrary from any script. This may be useful to recover from a * situation where a script forgot to call TriggersEnable at the end of * operation. In order to be suppressed, a script called via trigger should * use the TriggersAreActive function to decide whether or not to run. * * RETURNS: "" (null) * * PARAMETERS: none * * REFERENCED VARIABLES: * $$~DISABLETRIGGERS (deprecated) * $$~TRIGGERS_DISABLE * $$~TRIGGERS_SCRIPTS * $~triggers_disableKey * * DEPENDENCIES: none * * RELEASE: 2013-04-08 * * REFERENCES: * Supressible Triggered Scripts Best Practice: http://filemakerstandards.org/display/bp/Suppressible+Triggered+Scripts * ===================================== */ Let ( [ $$~TRIGGERS_SCRIPTS = "" ; $$~TRIGGERS_DISABLE = "" ; $$~DISABLETRIGGERS = "" ; $~triggers_disableKey = "" ] ; "" )
